target.name: Etched
target.founder: Gavin Uberti, Chris Zhu, Robert Wachen

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $5.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $500M - $2.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 20.3x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: Positron AI
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series B), post-money $1B+

data.narrative:
Etched is a Series B chip design company that has raised $620M+ and carries a post-money valuation of $5.0B. The valuation universe comprises five private-sector peers in chips and semiconductors at same or adjacent stages (d-Matrix at $2.0B Series C, Positron AI at $1.0B Series B, Substrate at $1.0B Series A, DEEPX at $529M Series C, and Rain AI at $500M Series A), alongside three public semiconductor comps (ALAB, NVDA, AMD).

Two valuation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement to the $5.0B base, implying $9.5B. Peer-median anchoring across the five same-sector comparables yields a median of $1.0B, with a range of $500M to $2.0B. Public-comp anchoring via price-to-sales multiples (sector median 20.3x across three tickers) cannot be applied without disclosed target revenue.

Triangulating the two applicable methods across their implied ranges produces a valuation band of $850M to $10.9B, with a median of $5.2B. The wide dispersion reflects material tension between peer medians—which suggest modest valuation relative to current round—and stage-progression mechanics, which support substantial step-up potential. Key uncertainties include the target's revenue trajectory, capital efficiency relative to peers, and the sustainability of Series B-to-C multiples in an unsettled semiconductor financing environment.
